_comment: Ground truth annotation — PRISMA checklist for Strohmeier et al. 2014 Cochrane Review

article.title: Antibiotics for acute pyelonephritis in children
article.authors: Strohmeier Y, Hodson EM, Willis NS, Webster AC, Craig JC

study_type.notes: Cochrane intervention review on antibiotic regimens for acute pyelonephritis in children. Includes multiple comparisons and meta-analyses.

1.notes: Title clearly identifies as '[Intervention Review] Antibiotics for acute pyelonephritis in children'

2.notes: Structured abstract with BACKGROUND, OBJECTIVES, SEARCH STRATEGY, SELECTION CRITERIA, DATA COLLECTION AND ANALYSIS, MAIN RESULTS, and AUTHORS' CONCLUSIONS

3.notes: Background describes UTIs in children, pyelonephritis, current treatment approaches, and need for systematic evaluation of antibiotic regimens

4.notes: Explicit objectives: to evaluate efficacy and safety of different antibiotic regimens for acute pyelonephritis in children

5.notes: Inclusion criteria: RCTs and quasi-RCTs of antibiotics for acute pyelonephritis in children. Exclusion: studies of prophylaxis or prevention

6.notes: Cochrane Central Register of Controlled Trials (CENTRAL), MEDLINE, EMBASE, and handsearching of journals searched

7.notes: Full search strategies provided with MeSH terms, text words, and Boolean operators for each database

8.notes: Two reviewers independently assessed studies for inclusion using standardized criteria. Disagreements resolved by consensus or third reviewer

9.notes: Two reviewers independently extracted data using standardized data extraction forms. Forms piloted before use

10.notes: Outcomes clearly specified: primary outcomes include persistent bacteriuria, fever resolution, kidney damage; secondary outcomes include recurrent UTI

11.notes: Cochrane risk of bias tool used to assess: sequence generation, allocation concealment, blinding, incomplete outcome data, selective outcome reporting, other bias

12.notes: Effect measures specified: risk difference for primary comparisons, odds ratios or risk ratios used throughout meta-analyses

13a.notes: Random-effects models described with Mantel-Haenszel method for pooling. Criteria for synthesis eligibility clearly stated

13b.notes: Statistical heterogeneity assessed using Cochran's Q test and I-squared (I²) statistic. I² > 50% considered substantial heterogeneity

14.notes: Funnel plots generated to assess publication bias for main outcomes

15.notes: GRADE approach mentioned for assessing quality of evidence for primary comparisons

16.notes: PRISMA flow diagram (Figure 1) showing: studies identified, screened, assessed for eligibility, and included in review

17.notes: Table of characteristics of included studies providing study design, population, interventions, and outcomes for each study

18.notes: Risk of bias assessment results presented with risk of bias summary figure and individual study assessments

19.notes: Forest plots (Figures 2-3) presenting meta-analysis results with pooled estimates and 95% confidence intervals for main outcomes

20.notes: Publication bias results presented via funnel plots for main analyses

21.notes: Summary of findings table provided with GRADE assessment of evidence quality

22a.notes: General interpretation: no definitive evidence for superiority of specific antibiotic regimens for uncomplicated acute pyelonephritis

22b.notes: Discussion of limitations: heterogeneous populations, different clinical presentations, study quality concerns, limited long-term follow-up

22c.notes: Implications discussed: need for further high-quality RCTs, standardized outcome measures, long-term follow-up studies

23.notes: Registration noted in Cochrane Renal Group with publication in Issue 7, 2014

24.notes: No funding information explicitly stated for the Cochrane review itself

25.notes: No conflicts of interest statement provided for the authors

26.notes: No statement about data availability or supplementary materials
